author: Maya (mum)
title: Tooth-brushing meltdown again
text: Third night this week. She screamed the electric brush was 'drilling her teeth'. Ended up with the soft manual one and a song. 20 min to do a 2-min job. I'm wrecked.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Calm morning — noted it
text: Ate breakfast, got dressed first try, walked to school holding my hand. Told her I was proud. She shrugged but smiled. Writing it down so I remember it's possible.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Appetite gone on the stim
text: Day 9 of methylphenidate. She picked at dinner, left most of it. Growth chart is in the back of my mind. Will flag with Dr Kostas at the review.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Sensory win — new headphones
text: Big W had the kid-size noise-cancelling ones on sale. She wore them in the shopping centre and actually walked next to the trolley. No meltdown. These are staying in the bag.

author: Maya (mum)
title: School gate drama
text: Something happened with Amelia at pickup. She wouldn't talk about it in the car. Cried for 10 min once we got home, then asked for a hug. Didn't push her.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Screen time transition
text: iPad off at 4pm. Usual explosion. 12 minutes of escalation. New plan from Jess: 5-min countdown with a visual timer. Will try tomorrow.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Timer worked
text: Visual timer for iPad. Two small protests, then handed it over. Told Jess this worked.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Bedtime — good one
text: Weighted blanket + story + red light. Asleep by 8:40. That is early for her.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Rainy day = bouncing off walls
text: Stuck inside. Ran laps in the hallway. Trampoline would have fixed this in 10 minutes. Need an indoor plan B.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Refused dinner — broccoli
text: Cried at the texture. Ate rice plain. I don't know if this is sensory or stim appetite loss. Feels like both.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Kind to Jamie
text: Shared her dinosaur puzzle with her little brother without being asked. He's 3. She's been rough with him lately. Wanted to write it down.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Footy grand final — overstimulated
text: Took her to a family lunch with the game on loud TV, 12 people, all adults chatting. Lasted 40 min before a full meltdown. Left early. Lesson: headphones next time, and an exit plan.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Homework — reading
text: 10 minutes of reading the sight word list. She wanted to stop after 3. I let her. Jess said picking battles.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Q for Jess next week
text: She's been chewing her shirt collars again. Two tops ruined. Is there a chewy I can get her for school? Ask Jess.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Morning routine chart
text: Put up the visual chart Jess made. She ticked boxes herself. Got out the door 8 min earlier. Skeptical it'll last but noting it.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Saturday was hard
text: Birthday party at a trampoline park. She wanted to go, then shut down at the door from the noise. Sat in the car for 20 min. Went in with headphones, lasted 30 min, left. She cried the whole way home because she 'wanted to be normal'. Gutted.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Med dose question
text: She said the stim makes her feel 'tight in her chest'. Probably nothing but I want to ask Dr Kostas.

author: Maya (mum)
title: School said she had a good day
text: Ms Harper emailed. 'Great focus in literacy today.' Screenshotted it, put it on the fridge.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Sleep onset — 90 min
text: Took her 90 minutes to fall asleep. Third time this week. Wonder if it's the afternoon dose timing.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Swimming lesson — yes
text: She LOVED it. Water + pressure + structure. This is our thing. Booking every Saturday.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Forgot headphones
text: Coles at 5pm with no headphones was a catastrophic planning error. I'll know for next time.

author: Maya (mum)
title: OT chewy arrived
text: Necklace-style chewy from Amazon. She's been wearing it to school. Collars are safe this week.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Emotion word — used 'frustrated'
text: Amit has been working on emotion labels. She said 'I'm frustrated with Jamie' instead of hitting him. Real thing. Writing it down.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Christmas concert — left early
text: She sang 2 songs then covered her ears and walked off stage. Ms Harper handled it beautifully. I was proud she lasted as long as she did.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Med holiday plan
text: Dr Kostas said a short med break over Christmas is OK. We'll try 4 days off, see what happens.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Day 2 off meds
text: Bouncing, loud, impulsive — all back. But eating like a horse. Two sides to this coin.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Day 4 off meds — restart Monday
text: She said 'my brain is too fast today'. Self-aware. Restarting Monday as planned.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Christmas morning — survived
text: Too many presents, too fast. 40 min meltdown at 10am. Retreated to her room with the weighted blanket. Came out at lunch like nothing happened.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Beach trip — unexpected win
text: Torquay for the day. Hours in the sand. Digging holes, no meltdowns. Heavy work + outdoors + space = gold.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Holiday is wearing thin
text: Too unstructured. She's snappy, I'm snappy. Back to school can't come fast enough. Fought with Jamie over a Lego piece for 45 minutes.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Year 2 anxiety
text: Started asking if the new teacher would be 'mean'. Couldn't sleep. We made a visit plan to see the classroom before term starts.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Classroom visit — helped
text: Went in with Mr Doyle. She chose where she wants to sit (near the window, away from the door). Much calmer after.

author: Maya (mum)
title: First day of Year 2
text: Walked in with her chewy, headphones in the bag, morning dose on time. Held it together. Came home shattered and silent. Bath + quiet = recovery.

author: Maya (mum)
title: She asked about sleepovers
text: Wants to stay at Amelia's. First time she's asked. I'm nervous about the unfamiliar environment. Will ask Amit.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Friday meltdown after school
text: She held it together all week and blew up at the front door at 3:15. Screaming, hitting the wall. 25 minutes. This is the pattern. She's masking all week.

author: Maya (mum)
title: School said it's going well
text: Mr Doyle emailed. She's using the sensory corner twice a day. Proud of her for asking.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Valentine's cards
text: Made cards for three kids. Specific about who got the pink one. Sweet.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Stomach ache — acute
text: Woke up at 2am crying with stomach pain. Lasted about an hour. GP in the morning. She's OK now but I'm watching.

author: Maya (mum)
title: GP visit
text: Dr Tran said likely constipation from the stim. Suggested more water and fibre. Flagged to Dr Kostas.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Friendship stuff — Amelia and Zara
text: Three-way dynamic. She says Zara 'took Amelia away'. Big feelings. Not sure how much to intervene.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Did her homework without a fight
text: First time all year. 20 minutes of reading. I didn't make a big deal of it. Writing it down.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Sleep onset trouble again
text: Back to 60-90 min to fall asleep. She's been asking to read later. Wondering if we're overdoing the afternoon dose.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Excursion to the zoo
text: Full school day, long bus, big crowds. Came home in pieces. Screamed at me for parking in the 'wrong spot'. I didn't. Recovery mode all evening.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Birthday coming up
text: She wants a small party. 4 kids, backyard, no music. Smart kid — she knows what she can handle.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Birthday party — went well
text: 4 kids, cake, bubbles in the backyard. No meltdowns. She asked for quiet time in her room twice and then came back out. Self-regulation.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Stomach again
text: Mild this time. Water + pear + walk. Resolved by dinner. Still tracking.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Spelling test — upset about mistakes
text: Got 8/10 and cried for 15 min about the two she missed. Perfectionism is real. Amit flagged this last session.

author: Maya (mum)
title: She told me she's 'wired different'
text: At bedtime. 'My brain is wired different to Jamie's.' We talked about it. She's proud of the 'fast' part, sad about the 'too much' part. She's 6 and saying this.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Holidays week 1 — OK
text: Kept routine tight. Swim Monday, park Tuesday, OT Wednesday, playdate Thursday. Structure is the whole game.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Play date sensory overload
text: Went to a kid's house with 3 dogs, a toddler and a loud TV. Lasted 40 min. She said 'too much' and asked to leave. I was impressed she asked.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Back to school in 5 days
text: Anxiety creeping back. She asked if the teacher would remember her. We looked at photos of Year 2 so far. Calmed her down.

author: Maya (mum)
title: Meltdown at the dentist
text: Bright lights, the chair reclining, the water suction noise. Couldn't do the clean. Dentist was patient. Will book an early-morning slot next time.

author: Dr A. Kostas (Paediatrician)
title: Commencement of methylphenidate — initial review
text: Ellie was reviewed today, two weeks after commencement of immediate-release methylphenidate 5 mg mane. Mother reports a noticeable reduction in impulsivity and improved task completion at home. Appetite suppression has been observed, with reduced intake at lunch and dinner. No cardiovascular symptoms. BP 98/64, HR 92, weight stable at 19.8 kg. Plan: continue 5 mg mane, add a 2.5 mg afternoon dose if school reports fragmented attention, review in 6 weeks. Mother counselled on appetite and sleep monitoring.

author: Dr R. Meyer (Child Psychiatrist)
title: Initial psychiatric assessment
text: Ellie, 6 years, presented with a strong clinical picture of combined-type ADHD with significant sensory sensitivities. Parent-reported symptoms include poor attention to non-preferred tasks, motor restlessness, impulsive interruption, and rigidity around transitions. School reports align. No features of anxiety disorder that exceed developmental norms at this time, though emergent perfectionism was noted. Formal DSM-5 criteria met for ADHD-C. Recommend continuation of stimulant with paediatrician-led titration and adjunct OT input for sensory regulation. Psychology input advisable for emotion regulation and anxiety-prevention work. No pharmacotherapy added at this stage.

author: Dr L. Tran (GP)
title: Referral — paediatric occupational therapy
text: Thank you for accepting Ellie for OT assessment. She is a 6-year-old with recently diagnosed ADHD-C and notable sensory sensitivities (auditory and tactile). Mother reports significant difficulty with grooming activities (tooth-brushing, hair-washing), transitions, and classroom regulation. Stimulant commenced by Dr Kostas. Goals for OT: sensory profiling, classroom regulation strategies, handwriting support if appropriate. Medicare CDM plan attached.

author: Dr A. Kostas (Paediatrician)
title: Medication titration note
text: Ellie reviewed at 6 weeks. School reports afternoon attention drop. Mother reports similar 2–3pm pattern at home on non-school days. Adding immediate-release methylphenidate 2.5 mg at midday. Continue 5 mg mane. Total daily dose 7.5 mg. BP 100/66, HR 88, weight 19.6 kg (minor loss, reviewing dietary intake). Plan: review in 4 weeks, family to trial a structured snack pre-morning dose.

author: Dr A. Kostas (Paediatrician)
title: Medication holiday — consent discussed
text: Family requests a short medication holiday over the Christmas break to assess behavioural baseline and support appetite recovery. Discussed risks (rebound symptoms, behavioural escalation, social disruption) and benefits (appetite, growth, clinician review of off-med baseline). Plan: 4 days off medication across 17–20 December, mother to log behavioural observations. Resume 7.5 mg split dose on 22 December.

author: Dr A. Kostas (Paediatrician)
title: Pre-Year 2 review
text: Ellie reviewed ahead of school return. Weight recovered to 20.3 kg over the break. BP and HR stable. Mother reports sleep onset has lengthened again in the past fortnight (60–90 min). Possible contributor: afternoon dose timing. Plan: keep 7.5 mg split dose (5 mg mane + 2.5 mg at 12:00) — aim to eliminate doses after 13:00. Continue OT, continue psychology. Review in 8 weeks.

author: Dr L. Tran (GP)
title: After-hours consult — abdominal pain
text: Ellie presented with an episode of acute mid-abdominal pain lasting approximately 60 minutes. No fever, no vomiting, no diarrhoea. On examination: soft, mildly tender periumbilically, no guarding, bowel sounds active. Differential includes constipation (likely — stimulant use, reduced dietary intake). Advised increased fluids, added fibre, gentle laxative trial, review if recurrent. No red flags at this time. Writing to Dr Kostas.

author: Dr A. Kostas (Paediatrician)
title: Consultant letter to school — reasonable adjustments
text: To whom it may concern at Brunswick Primary. Ellie is a current patient with a confirmed diagnosis of ADHD combined-type, with co-occurring sensory processing differences. She benefits from: (1) seating away from high-traffic doors and windows, (2) permitted access to noise-reducing headphones during assessments and assemblies, (3) permitted short sensory breaks (3–5 minutes) when requested, (4) scheduled movement opportunities. Stimulant medication is dosed prior to school and at midday; no further doses during school hours are required. Please liaise with me through the family for any queries.

author: Dr R. Meyer (Child Psychiatrist)
title: 6-month psychiatric review
text: Ellie reviewed at 6 months. Mother reports significant improvement in task completion, classroom participation, and emotion labelling (attributed to psychology input). Emergent perfectionism is a theme — school-based distress following minor errors. Sleep onset has returned to baseline since the afternoon dose was eliminated post-13:00. No current indication for additional pharmacotherapy. Recommend continuation of current regimen, continue psychology at current cadence, consider a single parent-directed session on perfectionism scripts. Next review 6 months or sooner if required.

author: Jess P. (OT)
title: Initial sensory profile
text: Completed Sensory Profile 2 (caregiver questionnaire) and clinic observation. Ellie scores in the 'much more than others' range for Sensory Sensitivity and Sensory Avoiding. Auditory and tactile channels are primary. Strong seeking behaviour for proprioceptive input (jumping, crashing, deep pressure). Plan: weekly sessions for 6 weeks. Goals: (1) classroom self-advocacy for sensory breaks, (2) morning routine checklist, (3) mother coached on proprioceptive 'heavy work' at home.

author: Jess P. (OT)
title: Session 2 — handwriting grip
text: Observed tripod grip with excessive pressure, fatigue after 5 minutes. Trialled weighted pencil and rubber grip — Ellie preferred the soft grip. Introduced pre-writing hand warm-up (theraputty). Took home a grip and a putty pot. Mother to do 2 min warm-up before homework.

author: Jess P. (OT)
title: Session 3 — iPad transition strategy
text: Worked on transitions from preferred screen activity to non-preferred tasks. Co-designed a visual countdown timer system with Ellie. She chose a dinosaur theme. Mother trialling at home. Next week: review what worked, iterate.

author: Amit S. (Psychologist)
title: Session 1 — emotion vocabulary
text: First session. Rapport-building via Lego. Introduced a feelings chart with six primary emotions and short descriptors. Ellie identified 'frustrated' and 'excited' readily; 'disappointed' and 'overwhelmed' took scaffolding. Homework: mother to label two feelings per day using the chart.

author: Jess P. (OT)
title: Session 4 — chewy trial
text: Parent query — collar chewing at school. Trialled two chewy necklaces in clinic. Ellie preferred the softer grey one. Ordered at home. Discussed school permission — liaison letter to be sent.

author: Amit S. (Psychologist)
title: Session 2 — emotion labelling progress
text: Mother reports Ellie spontaneously used 'frustrated' at home re sibling conflict — significant. In session, Ellie mapped a recent event (school concert) to three feelings in sequence (excited → overwhelmed → sad). Introduced basic regulation scripts. Progress positive.

author: Jess P. (OT)
title: Session 5 — classroom self-advocacy
text: Role-played the 'sensory break card' system with Ellie. She practised raising the card silently to Ms Harper. Liaison with Mr Doyle to embed in classroom. Goal for term 1 Year 2: autonomous break requests.

author: Dana K. (Speech Pathologist)
title: Pragmatic language — initial
text: Assessment session. Narrative and semantic language broadly age-appropriate. Pragmatic domain — difficulty maintaining topic in a 3-person conversation, interrupts peers, struggles with non-literal language ('pull your socks up'). Plan: 6 sessions focused on conversational repair and group dynamics. Coordinate with Amit re emotion overlap.

author: Amit S. (Psychologist)
title: Session 3 — sleepover anxiety
text: Mother reports Ellie requesting a sleepover at a peer's house. Unpacked in session. Anxiety primarily about unfamiliar sensory environment (lights, noises). Built a step-ladder: (1) afternoon playdate, (2) dinner and stay until 8pm, (3) full sleepover. Mother to coordinate with peer's parent.

author: Jess P. (OT)
title: Session 6 — Year 2 transition review
text: Mother reports classroom-visit strategy (pre-term) worked well. Ellie chose her own seat near a window. Discussed the first-week 'mask-crash' pattern at home and strategies for decompression. Recommend a post-school 'quiet window' of 30–45 minutes with no demands.

author: Amit S. (Psychologist)
title: Session 4 — after-school mask-crash
text: Explored the Friday afternoon meltdown pattern. Ellie identified that she 'holds it in' at school and it 'falls out' at home. Normalised and validated. Worked on giving the pattern a name ('the overflow') and planning a predictable after-school routine.

author: Dana K. (Speech Pathologist)
title: Pragmatic language — session 2
text: Worked on conversational repair: noticing when a peer has changed topic, asking rather than interrupting. Used puppet role-play. Ellie engaged well; generalisation to playground remains the work.

author: Amit S. (Psychologist)
title: Session 5 — perfectionism
text: Ellie presented distressed re a spelling test (8/10). Cognitive distortion identified ('I got it wrong = I am bad at spelling'). Introduced the 'OK-different-than-I-wanted' frame. Homework: mother to model the frame when she makes small errors.

author: Jess P. (OT)
title: Term 1 review
text: Ellie using the sensory corner at school autonomously 2x/day per teacher report. Chewy necklace embedded. Morning checklist being used inconsistently (mother reports drop-off after week 3 — common). Reset and iterated. Handwriting grip now sustained for 12 minutes.

author: Amit S. (Psychologist)
title: Session 6 — friendship dynamics
text: Three-way friendship distress (Ellie / Amelia / Zara). Worked on the script: 'I want to play too — can the three of us do X?'. Role-played with stuffed animals. Some resistance, came around. Homework: mother to coach script if it arises.

author: Jess P. (OT)
title: Holiday session — regulation review
text: Holiday session by request. Discussed which sensory strategies generalise to the unstructured holiday environment. Swimming identified as a high-yield activity (proprioceptive + structured). Recommend maintaining weekly swim block into term 2.

author: Amit S. (Psychologist)
title: Session 7 — self-identity
text: Ellie spontaneously discussed being 'wired different'. Affirmed. Explored what she likes about her 'fast brain' (ideas, energy) and what is hard (too much, too loud). Strength-based conversation. Mother reports Ellie initiated this at home too — positive signal.

author: Ms Harper (Year 1 teacher)
title: Quick update — good focus today
text: Hi Maya, just a quick note to say Ellie had a really focused literacy session today. She finished the task and read her story to the group. Wanted you to know. — Jo

author: Ms Harper (Year 1 teacher)
title: Pickup this afternoon
text: Maya, there was an incident at lunch between Ellie and Amelia. Nobody hurt, just some upset feelings around a game. Ellie was quiet in the afternoon and didn't want to talk about it. Flagging so you have context at pickup. — Jo

author: Mr Doyle (Learning support)
title: IEP meeting date
text: Hi Maya, proposing Tuesday 25th at 2:30pm for the IEP review. Ms Harper and I will attend. Let me know if that works. — Tom

author: Ms Harper (Year 1 teacher)
title: Good week
text: Just wanted to send a positive note — Ellie had a great week. Used her break card once on Thursday (very proud of her), finished all her maths, made a lovely card for Amelia's birthday. Jo

author: Mrs Ng (SENCO)
title: Sensory break card — embedded
text: Hi Maya, the break card is now part of Ellie's classroom routine across all teachers. She's used it 3–4 times this fortnight. Mr Doyle will continue to monitor. Let me know if anything from home suggests we need to adjust. — Priya

author: Ms Harper (Year 1 teacher)
title: Concert logistics
text: Quick one — we'll seat Ellie near the aisle so she can step out if the concert gets overwhelming. Mr Doyle will be nearby. Wanted you to know the plan. — Jo

author: Mr Doyle (Learning support)
title: Year 2 first week wrap
text: Hi Maya, solid first week. Ellie chose her seat, introduced herself to the new teacher calmly, and used the sensory corner once on Wednesday. She seems to like the more structured Year 2 routine. — Tom

author: Mrs Ng (SENCO)
title: Reasonable adjustments — paed letter received
text: Hi Maya, received Dr Kostas's letter. Adjustments are already in place. I've circulated to Ellie's Year 2 teacher and specialist teachers (PE, music, art). No action needed from you. — Priya

author: Ms Harper (Year 1 teacher, now Year 2 coordinator)
title: Friendship update
text: Hi Maya, noticed Ellie has had some friction with Zara this week around a three-way dynamic with Amelia. Nothing escalated, but she was quiet at Thursday lunch. Flagging so you can check in at home. — Jo

author: Mr Doyle (Learning support)
title: Excursion prep — zoo
text: Hi Maya, zoo excursion is Monday. Bus leaves 9am, back 3pm. Ellie will have her headphones and chewy. I'll be her point of contact. Long day — she may be flat afterwards. — Tom

author: Ms Harper
title: Spelling test feedback
text: Maya, Ellie had a hard time with the spelling test result on Friday (8/10 — strong score). She was upset about the two she missed. I gave her space in the sensory corner and she rejoined the group. Might be worth mentioning to Amit. — Jo

author: Mrs Ng (SENCO)
title: Term 1 wrap — IEP summary
text: Hi Maya, end-of-term summary: Ellie has met 3/4 IEP goals (sensory self-advocacy, morning routine, task completion). Goal 4 (peer conflict repair) is partially met — progressing. IEP review next term will focus there. Brief attached. — Priya

author: Mr Doyle (Learning support)
title: Welcome back — term 2
text: Hi Maya, ready for Ellie to start term 2 on Monday. Same seat, same plan. Let me know if anything shifted over the holidays that I should know about before then. — Tom
